images.caption: Figure 1:TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}enables precise synthesis usingpolygons,polylines,bounding boxes,points, along with text and geolocation conditioning.

Figure 2:Geospatial Primitive Conditions.Examples of native geospatial primitives{polygon, polyline, bounding box, point}\{\text{polygon, polyline, bounding box, point}\}. Polygons and polylines are represented as coordinate sequences, bounding boxes as corner pairs, and points as single coordinates.

Figure 3:TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}Architecture.A Unified Primitive Encoder encodes primitives for MetaRBF+, which predicts Gaussian kernel parameters (σx,σy,θ\sigma_{x},\sigma_{y},\theta), modulated by a Spatial Geometry Field. The resulting signal regularizes attention between primitive and visual tokens through Geometry-Aware Local Attention.

Figure 4:Geometry-Aware Local Attention (GALA).Unified primitive tokens cross-attend with visual tokens, which are then multiplicatively modulated by a Gaussian prior and, for complex primitives, a spatial geometry field.

Figure 5:Qualitative Samples from TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}.Conditions increase in complexity and annotation budget from top to bottom, while contextual modalities (global text and geolocation) are progressively added from left to right.Polygonsdenote ‘building’,polylinesdenote ‘road’,bounding boxesdenote ‘recreational’, andpointsdenote ‘trees’.
Figure 6:Qualitative comparison with geospatial grounding models.

Figure 7:Comparison of Formats.Spatial alignment improves with richer conditioning.Polylinesbetter preserve the highway direction (left) andpolygonsalign more tightly with the warehouse building (top right). Imagery is best viewed zoomed in.
Figure 8:Structural similarity with real imagery improves as the annotation budget scales. SSIM (↑\uparrow) is evaluated on Git-Dense-3.5k.

Table 1:Zero-shot evaluation on Git-Rand-15k, Git-Spatial-15k, and Git-Dense-3.5k.↓\downarrowindicates lower is better, while↑\uparrowindicates higher is better.TTis text input,OOis OSM raster input,LLis geolocation,PPis points,BBis bounding box, andΩ\Omegais {polygon, polyline, bounding box, and point conditioning}.*Text2Earth was trained on the entire Git-10M dataset (including our test sets), which may inflate reported performance.

Figure 9:Qualitative Comparison between Geometry-Aware Local Attention (GALA), Adaptive Local Attention (ALA)[sastry2026terradit], Instance Masked Attention (IMA)[wang2024instancediffusion], Gated Self-Attention (GSA)[li2023gligen], and Cross Attention (Cross) after 400k steps. Our approach (GALA), exhibits much stronger structural fidelity compared to other methods.

Figure 10:Synthetic data augmentation across 4 downstream tasks.(Top)TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}synthetic samples on (a) DIOR, (b) OpenEarthMap, (c) City-Scale, (d) AID.(Bottom)We use RT-DETR[zhao2024detrs]for object detection (DIOR), U-Net[ronneberger2015u]for land cover segmentation (OpenEarthMap), SAM-Road[hetang2024segment]for road graph extraction (City-Scale), and a ResNet-34[he2016deep]for scene classification (AID).
Table S1:Dataset split statistics. Distribution of images, instances, and tags across the training and testing splits for TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}.
Figure S2:Train and Test Splits.We show the geolocation distribution and random image samples for each split.

Table S3:Model Details and Zero-shot evaluation on Git-Rand-15k, Git-Spatial-15k, and Git-Dense-3.5k.↑\uparrowindicates higher is better.TTis text input,OOis OSM raster input,LLis geolocation,PPis points,BBis bounding box, andΩ\Omegais {polygon, polyline, bounding box, and point conditioning}.*Text2Earth was trained on the entire Git-10M dataset (including our test sets), which may inflate reported performance.
Figure S3:Comparison with baseline approaches on zero-shot evaluation on Git-Rand-15k. Our approach exhibits best FID with comparable parameter count.
Figure S6:TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}-XL Uncurated Samples. Residential and river in Quebec, Canada.
Figure S8:TerraDiT-Ω\mathrm{\Omega}-XL Uncurated Samples. Rural area in Tilly, France.
Figure S10:Qualitative Comparisons with Geospatial Models.

Figure S11:More Comparisons of Attention Methods (B/2 Models). GALA is our Geometry-Aware Local Attention, ALA is Adaptive Local Attention[sastry2026terradit], IMA is Instance Masked Attention[wang2024instancediffusion], GSA is Gated Self-Attention[li2023gligen], and Cross is vanilla cross-attention.
